plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs to eliminate extreme risk.
Reduce exposure in QQQI and CRWD to lower concentration.
Sell SMH to significantly cut semiconductor exposure.
Increase SPY to build a larger, stable index base.
Add AMD as a new, high-quality long position.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation with reduced leverage.
conservative.read: This mode aims for lower, steadier gains around 1.5% per month. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration risk, focusing on stability and capital preservation.

plan: Trim SOXL and sell SPAL to reduce overall leverage.
Reduce QQQI and SMH to lessen sector concentration.
Increase SPY to meet concentration limits and add stability.
Add AMD and ANET from the scanner as new positions.
This plan balances growth with risk reduction.
It aims for moderate growth around 2.5% monthly with controlled risk.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets about 2.5% monthly growth. It reduces high leverage and concentration while adding quality names, aiming for risk-first growth.

plan: Increase SOXL and SPAL exposure to leverage upside potential.
Sell QQQI to remove income focus and free up capital.
Increase SMH and add AMD aggressively to concentrate in tech/semis.
Add CEG as a new high-conviction setup.
Reduce SPY slightly to manage overall concentration.
This plan aims for aggressive growth around 4% monthly, accepting higher risk.
aggressive.read: This aggressive mode targets ~4% monthly growth by leaning into leverage and high-scoring tech/semiconductor setups. Expect higher potential gains but also significantly larger drawdowns and the risks of leveraged ETF decay.

moves.note: Hold for potential future income play

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs and volatile single names like SMH and CRWD.
Significantly increase QQQI to maximize income generation.
Increase SPY for stability and broad market exposure.
Hold AMD as a watch item, not adding to it now.
This plan prioritizes stable monthly income and capital preservation.
It aims for reliable distributions, not aggressive growth.
income.read: This income-focused mode targets around 1% monthly distributions. It prioritizes stable income-generating ETFs and broad index funds while cutting leverage and volatile growth stocks to minimize drawdown risk.
